full_prompt: Design one representative interior in-home residential tornado safe room, 2.4 m x 2.4 m x 2.4 m clear interior, built as a site-cast reinforced concrete box (four walls + roof slab + floor slab) compliant with FEMA P-320 Taking Shelter from the Storm (5th ed., 2021). The room must protect occupants from an EF5 tornado event with a 250 mph (112 m/s) 3-second gust design wind speed, a design wind pressure derived from that gust on an Exposure C site with directionality and gust factors per ICC 500 / ASCE 7, and the tornado debris missile defined in FEMA P-320 and ICC 500: a 15-lb (6.8 kg) nominal Douglas-fir 2x4 lumber impactor traveling at 100 mph (44.7 m/s) horizontally against walls and 67 mph (30.0 m/s) vertically against the roof, impacting at any location. All reinforced-concrete walls and roof must be 6.0-8.0 in (152-203 mm) thick with normal-weight concrete of minimum compressive strength 4000 psi (27.6 MPa) and Grade 60 reinforcement (fy = 420 MPa) detailed per the FEMA P-320 prescriptive schedule (e.g. #4 bars each way each face, or equivalent wall steel ratio). The entire safe room must be anchored to a continuous reinforced concrete foundation with anchor bolts sized and embedded to resist the full uplift and overturning from the 250 mph wind with a net safety factor of at least 1.0 against ASCE 7 load-factored demand. The single entry door must be a tested FEMA ICC 500 compliant safe-room door assembly; the door itself is NOT designed in this sample (listed as excluded) but the rough opening and its reinforced jamb must be modeled. Deliverable is a single assembled CAD model of the RC shell with rebar cage representation, foundation, and anchor layout, suitable for (a) linear + nonlinear static structural FEA under design wind pressure, (b) explicit-dynamics debris-impact analysis of the 2x4 missile against the wall and roof, and (c) anchor pull-out / overturning check. Vibration (modal), thermal, fluid (CFD of wind), and radiation analyses are NOT required; wind is applied as an equivalent static pressure. The 2x4 missile impact explicitly requires a non-FEA static solver category (explicit-dynamics / transient-dynamics solver such as LS-DYNA, Abaqus/Explicit, or Radioss); it cannot be evaluated with linear static FEA alone.
short_prompt:
Design one interior in-home residential tornado safe room as a site-cast reinforced-concrete box (four walls + roof slab + floor slab on a continuous RC footing with anchor bolts) per FEMA P-320 Taking Shelter from the Storm (5th ed., 2021) and ICC 500. Normal-weight concrete with minimum 4000 psi fc and Grade 60 rebar per the prescriptive schedule; tested ICC 500 door assembly and reinforced jamb (door itself out of scope).

Envelope: ~2.4 x 2.4 x 2.4 m clear interior, 6-8 in RC walls and roof.

Load cases.
- LC1 design wind: EF5-equivalent 250 mph 3-sec gust on Exposure C, applied as ASCE 7 / ICC 500 equivalent static pressures on walls and roof.
- LC2 debris missile horizontal: 15 lb (6.8 kg) Douglas-fir 2x4 impacting any wall at 100 mph.
- LC3 debris missile vertical: same 2x4 impacting the roof at 67 mph.
- Anchors and overall overturning must resist ASCE 7 factored wind uplift / overturning demand.

Debris-missile impact requires an explicit-dynamics solver; the wind case is static.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: The 15-lb 2x4 debris missile at 100 mph horiz / 67 mph vert is a transient impact event; penetration / perforation must be evaluated with explicit dynamics, not linear static FEA.

prompt.title: FEMA P-320 interior residential tornado safe room
prompt.brief: Design one 2.4 x 2.4 x 2.4 m interior RC safe room for 250 mph gust + 15-lb 2x4 at 100/67 mph debris missile per FEMA P-320.

load_cases.name: design_wind
load_cases.application: ASCE 7 / ICC 500 derived pressures on walls + roof

load_cases.name: debris_missile_horizontal
load_cases.application: normal impact on any wall location

boundary_conditions.foundation_interface: continuous RC footing, anchored with bolts per prescriptive schedule
boundary_conditions.door_opening: reinforced jamb; door assembly itself tested separately

objective.secondary: minimum wall thickness within 6-8 in range

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: FEMA P-320 Table for EF5 shelter
ACI 318 phi*Mn >= Mu under ASCE 7 factored load combos
FEMA P-320 / ICC 500 Section 305 debris-impact test criterion
ACI 318 Appendix D / Ch. 17 factored demand vs anchor capacity
ASCE 7 factored load combination 0.9D vs wind uplift + overturning

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid (concrete), embedded 1D rebar (reinforcement), rigid impactor body

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: P-320 safe rooms are not evaluated against modal/harmonic loads.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: No thermal envelope requirement in P-320.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Wind is reduced to ICC 500 equivalent static pressures; no CFD.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Short thick RC walls not buckling-governed.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Door assembly qualification (tested to ICC 500 separately)

eval_coverage: explicit-dynamics debris-impact coupled with static wind FEA
FEMA P-320 exact numeric loads (250 mph / 100 mph / 67 mph)
anchor pull-out and overturning against ASCE 7 factored combos
